provenance.pin_detail: no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now)

privilege_notes: runs fetched package code at launch

findings.title: Installed on-the-fly via a package runner
findings.detail: Server 'mcp-gitlab' is fetched and executed at launch via 'npx' with auto-accept flag(s) -y; package spec: @zereight/mcp-gitlab.
findings.recommendation: Install the server as a pinned dependency and invoke the local binary, or vendor it. If a runner is unavoidable, pin an exact version (see unpinned_source).

findings.title: Package source is unpinned or mutable
findings.detail: Server 'mcp-gitlab' package spec '@zereight/mcp-gitlab' is unpinned (pin strength: none): no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now).
findings.recommendation: Pin an exact version (pkg@1.2.3) or a git commit SHA. Avoid @latest and branch refs.

evidence.reason: no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now)

findings.title: Server requires sensitive credentials
findings.detail: Server 'mcp-gitlab' requires credential-like env keys: GITLAB_AUTH_COOKIE_PATH, GITLAB_JOB_TOKEN, GITLAB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN, NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED.
findings.recommendation: Confirm each credential is scoped to least privilege and rotatable. Note which tools can exfiltrate it.

evidence.snippet: th where to create/update the file Content of the file Commit message Br

tools.name: get_file_blame
tools.description: Get git blame for a file at a given ref. Each entry maps a contiguous range of source lines to the commit that last changed them (id, author, authored_date, message). Use range_start/range_end to limit blame to specific lines.

candidate_capabilities.title: Filesystem read

evidence.snippet: get_file_blame get file blame

candidate_capabilities.rationale: Reading file contents is the most common SOURCE half of an exfiltration chain — pair it with any network_egress tool and the server can read a secret/config/document and send it out. tools.name: discover_tools
tools.description: Discover and activate additional tool categories for this session. Available categories: merge_requests, issues, repositories, branches, projects, labels, ci, groups, pipelines, milestones, wiki, releases, tags, users, workitems, webhooks, search. Already-active categories are listed in the response.

toxic_combinations.title: Read-then-send exfiltration chain
toxic_combinations.detail: The server can both access secrets/credentials and make outbound network calls — a path to exfiltrate them to an attacker-chosen host.

toxic_combinations.title: Arbitrary file read paired with egress
toxic_combinations.detail: The server can read file/message contents and make outbound network calls — a read-then-send exfiltration path. The read accepts an arbitrary path (or the server is scoped to a broad directory), so it can reach secrets/configs far beyond the task.

validated_out.reason: Merging within GitLab API (internal operation); 'URL' match is from generic description of project path encoding, not external network parameter.
Matched 'message' in 'merge_commit_message' refers to git commit message, not communications/email/chat content.
Internal GitLab operation; 'URL' match is generic project path description.
Tool creates/updates files (write operation), not reads them; matched pattern is about file content being written.
'message' in 'commit_message' refers to git commit message, not communications content.
'directory' in context refers to filesystem directory in Git, not address book/contacts directory.
Tool pushes (writes) files, not reads them.
'message' in 'commit_message' refers to git commit message, not communications.
'members' in description means project members with commit access, not a communications roster/participant list.
'public' in description refers to filtering confidential vs public visibility, not aggregate public data stats.
'token' in 'Reduces token usage' refers to API token rate limiting/quotas, not a secrets access parameter.
'member' in 'where current user is a member' refers to project membership/access control, not communications roster.
'page_token' is a pagination token, not a secrets access parameter or API credential.
'message' in commit blame refers to git commit message, not communications/email.
'directory' in 'local_path' refers to filesystem directory for saving files, not address book/contacts.
